entries.value: Target member retention rate is >80% annually. Industry average is 72%. Below 70% signals onboarding or engagement problems. Track 30/60/90-day retention separately to catch early churn.
Target class utilization is 70% of max capacity on average. Below 50% means the class may need schedule changes or marketing. Above 90% consistently means add another session to reduce waitlists.
Average revenue per member (ARPM) benchmark for boutique studios: $120-200/month. Includes membership fees plus ancillary revenue (retail, personal training, workshops). Track monthly trend.
Monthly membership churn rate target: <5% (equivalent to <46% annual). Churn above 7%/month is critical. Segment by plan type — class packs churn higher than unlimited memberships.
Peak hours (6-8 AM, 5-7 PM weekdays) should run at 75-90% capacity. Below 60% during peaks means pricing or programming issues. Above 95% means members can't book and may churn.
Alert when no-show rate exceeds 15% for any class. High no-shows waste instructor time and block waitlisted members. Consider late-cancel fees or waitlist auto-fill policies.
Flag members with payments past due >7 days. Auto-pause membership at 14 days past due. Cancel at 30 days. Past-due rate above 5% of active members signals a billing system problem.
New members should attend at least 3 classes in their first 14 days. Members who hit this milestone have 2.5x higher 6-month retention. Trigger outreach if no check-in within 7 days of joining.
Instructor-to-participant ratio: 1:15 for group fitness, 1:8 for specialized (yoga, pilates), 1:20 for cycling. Exceeding these ratios degrades experience and increases injury risk.

Membership stages: Active (current, paid up) -> Paused (temporary freeze, max 90 days) -> Past Due (payment failed) -> Cancelled (member-initiated) -> Expired (term ended, no renewal). Track transitions for churn analysis.
